items.correct: Longshore drift carries sediment past a change in coastline direction (e.g. river mouth); deposition continues into deeper water, building a curved depositional landform with a hook.
items.misconception: A spit forms where the river deposits its sediment at the coast.
items.random_wrong: A spit is a small island just off the coast.
items.why: Spits are coastal (longshore drift) deposits, not river deposits. The hook forms where wind shifts the current. Students confuse spits with deltas — different processes, different settings.

items.q: What is the difference between a megacity and a world city?
items.correct: A megacity is defined by population (over 10 million); a world city is defined by global economic and cultural influence (e.g. New York, London) — so a city can be one without the other.
items.misconception: Megacities and world cities are the same thing.
items.random_wrong: A megacity has over 1 million people and a world city has over 5 million.
items.why: Dhaka and Kinshasa are megacities but not world cities; Zurich is a world city but not a megacity. The distinction is quantitative (size) vs qualitative (function and global connections).

items.correct: The combined loss of water from a surface through evaporation (from soil and water bodies) and transpiration (from plant leaves).
items.misconception: It is only water evaporating from puddles and lakes.
items.random_wrong: It is the process of rain falling from clouds.
items.why: Students focus only on evaporation. Transpiration from forests can move enormous quantities of water — a key concept for understanding deforestation's effect on the water cycle.
It's Hess. “Wegener” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: What was the Tohoku earthquake magnitude?
items.why: It's 9.0. “7.0” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How tall (m) was the Tohoku tsunami?
items.why: The real figure is 15 m. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
